title: A joint resolution providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Environmental Protection Agency relating to "Trichloroethylene (TCE); Regulation Under the Toxic Substances Control Act (TSCA)".
plain_summary: This joint resolution would cancel a new EPA rule about a chemical called trichloroethylene or TCE. The EPA rule was published in December 2024 and bans companies from making, importing, or selling TCE for any purpose. The rule also stops businesses from using TCE in their operations. If Congress passes this resolution, the EPA rule would be thrown out and could never be put back in place. This means companies could continue to make and use TCE like they did before the EPA rule.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.

sections.title: Joint Resolution
sections.text: Congress has decided to reject a rule that the Environmental Protection Agency created about a chemical called Trichloroethylene, also known as TCE. The EPA made this rule under a law called the Toxic Substances Control Act, which is a law that controls dangerous chemicals. The EPA published this rule in the Federal Register on December seventeenth, two thousand twenty-four. Congress is using its power under chapter eight of title five of the United States Code to disapprove this rule. This means the rule will not have any legal power and cannot be enforced.
